raw_text:

{
  "claims": [
    "Californians pay more money in taxes to the Federal government than we get back in services or assistance.",
    "Diaper costs can exceed $100 per month per child, creating a financial burden for many California families.",
    "More than 1 in 4 households with children are facing hunger.",
    "Diapers are one of the most frequently requested non-food items at food banks and charitable organizations.",
    "A steady supply of diapers is vital for children's healthy development.",
    "Access to a steady supply of diapers is necessary for parents struggling to make ends meet to access child care and be able to work.",
    "Under AB 636, children enrolled in Medi-Cal who have a prescription for diapers can receive them at no cost through a federal program within Medicaid."
  ]
